ucs.title: [LLM] APT31 / Violet Typhoon: msiexec MSI install-from-URL spawned by Office or browser post-spearphish click
ucs.description: Hunts ZIRCONIUM's signature delivery chain where a spearphishing link (T1566.002 / T1598.003) leads to user execution (T1204.001) of an MSI payload retrieved over HTTP(S) via msiexec.exe (T1218.007). APT31 has repeatedly used this living-off-the-land sideload combo to drop their custom DropboxAES / DropDoor implants while bypassing AppLocker.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=msiexec.exe (Processes.process="*/i*http*" OR Processes.process="*/q*http*" OR Processes.process="* /package http*") (Processes.parent_process_name IN (outlook.exe, chrome.exe, msedge.exe, firefox.exe, iexplore.exe, winword.exe, excel.exe, powerpnt.exe, hh.exe)) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=cmdline "(?i)(?<msi_url>https?://[^\s\"]+\.msi)" | search msi_url=* | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let clicks = UrlClickEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed","UrlScanned")
| project ClickTime=Timestamp, AccountUpn, ClickedUrl=Url, NetworkMessageId;
let remoteMsi = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b(/i|/package|/quiet|/qn)\b.*https?://"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","hh.exe")
| extend MsiUrl = extract(@"(?i)(https?://[^\s\"]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountUpn, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine, MsiUrl;
remoteMsi
| join kind=leftouter clicks on AccountUpn
| where isnull(ClickTime) or (Timestamp between (ClickTime .. (ClickTime + 1h)))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountUpn, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine, MsiUrl, ClickedUrl
ucs.rationale: Microsoft (MSTIC) and Check Point both attribute the msiexec-from-URL pattern under Office/browser parents to ZIRCONIUM/APT31 election-targeting waves. Generic admin scripted MSI installs almost never originate from outlook.exe/winword.exe/chrome.exe parents, giving a very narrow attack-surface signature for this actor. Joining to UrlClickEvents ties the install back to the spearphishing-link init

ucs.title: [LLM] APT31 / Violet Typhoon: browser Login-Data theft followed by Dropbox API exfil (DropboxAES C2 pattern)
ucs.description: Hunts the ZIRCONIUM dual-stage tradecraft of stealing Chromium/Edge/Firefox credential stores (T1555.003) and exfiltrating to Dropbox via the actor's well-documented DropboxAES / DropBook implant family which abuses content.dropboxapi.com as a bidirectional C2 + exfil channel (T1102.002, T1567.002, T1573.001).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*\\Login Data*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data\\*\\Login Data*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\logins.json*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\key4.db*") (Filesystem.process_name!=chrome.exe Filesystem.process_name!=msedge.exe Filesystem.process_name!=firefox.exe Filesystem.process_name!=explorer.exe) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_guid Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename process_guid as cred_pguid dest as host | join type=inner host cred_pguid [| tstats summariesonly=true sum(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2 from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest="*.dropboxapi.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="content.dropboxapi.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="api.dropboxapi.com") (All_Traffic.app!=Dropbox.exe All_Traffic.app!=chrome.exe All_Traffic.app!=msedge.exe All_Traffic.app!=firefox.exe) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_guid | rename All_Traffic.src as host All_Traffic.process_guid as cred_pguid] | where bytes_out > 5000 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let credSteal = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
  or FileName in~ ("Login Data","logins.json","key4.db","cookies.sqlite","Cookies")
| where FolderPath has_any (
    @"\Google\Chrome\User Data\",
    @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\",
    @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\",
    @"\BraveSoftware\Brave-Browser\User Data\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","OneDrive.exe","explorer.exe","MsMpEng.exe")
| project CredTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ThiefImage=InitiatingProcessFolderPath, ThiefName=InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ThiefPid=InitiatingProcessId, ThiefCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, FolderPath;
let dropboxBeacon = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteUrl has_any ("content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","api-content.dropbox.com")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("Dropbox.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","OneDrive.exe")
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP,
          ThiefName=InitiatingProcessFileName, ThiefPid=InitiatingProcessId;
credSteal
| join kind=inner dropboxBeacon on DeviceId, ThiefName
| where NetTime between (CredTime .. (CredTime + 2h))
| summarize CredFiles=make_set(FileName), DropboxHits=count(), FirstHit=min(CredTime), LastBeacon=max(NetTime)
          by DeviceName, AccountName, ThiefImage, ThiefName, ThiefCmd
| where DropboxHits > 0
ucs.rationale: ZIRCONIUM/APT31's DropboxAES (a.k.a. DropBook) family is one of the most distinctive elements of the cluster — Microsoft and Check Point's 2020-2021 reporting both highlight Dropbox-API abuse for AES-encrypted bidirectional C2 and exfil. Pairing browser Login Data / key4.db touches by a non-browser process with Dropbox-API egress from the same process lineage, within a short window, is a near-bina
